rss.title: Germany troop cuts send wrong signal to Russia, say two top US Republicans
rss.snippet: The chairs of the House and Senate armed services committees say withdrawing 5,000 service personnel risked undermining deterrence.

rss.title: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
rss.snippet: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.

rss.title: The clandestine network smuggling Starlink tech into Iran to beat internet blackout

arxiv_papers.title: Social Media Data Toolkit: Standardization and Anonymization of Social Network Datasets
arxiv_papers.snippet: The rapid diversification of social media platforms and the increasing restrictions on official APIs have significantly complicated cross-platform analysis. Researchers are often forced to rely on heterogeneous datasets obtained through web scraping and historical archives; however they often lack s

arxiv_papers.title: Temporal and Content Coupling Analysis of Social Media User Behavior
arxiv_papers.snippet: News consumption behavior is shaped by the coupling between temporal dynamics and content selection. This study proposes a multi-scale temporal-content framework and validates it on two large real-world news datasets, MIND and Adressa. Results reveal hierarchical temporal patterns. podcast: [Snail Sex Tape] In this episode, we consider a creature we often don’t think much about—the snail. And not just snails, but their sex lives. Which, as it turns out, is epic. There is persuasion and subterfuge, spaghetti penises and co-copulation. And this very surprising habit—erm kink—of making tiny arrows (actually!) and stabbing each other with them.
